        performLayout
      • performLayout(r: RenderAlign)
      • performLayout(r: RenderAspectRatio)
      • performLayout(r: RenderConstrainedBox)
      • performLayout(r: RenderPadding)
      • performLayout(r: RenderProxyBox)
      • performLayout(r: RenderSizedBox)

Proxy-style render objects: single-child render objects that mostly delegate to their child. These are the internal render-tree nodes behind widgets like Padding, Center, Align, ConstrainedBox, Opacity, Transform, SizedBox, AspectRatio, ClipRect, ClipRRect, ColoredBox.

End users typically don't construct these directly; they use the widget constructors in flit/widgets/basic.nim which create the right render object via their createRenderObject method.

Imports

../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color

Types

RenderAlign = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  alignment*: Alignment
  widthFactor*: float32
  heightFactor*: float32
Positions the child according to alignment. When widthFactor/heightFactor are nonzero, sizes itself to child.size * factor; otherwise fills its constraints. Backs the Align and center widgets. 
RenderAspectRatio = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  aspectRatio*: float32
Sizes the child to a given width/height ratio while obeying parent constraints. Backs the AspectRatio widget. 
RenderClipRect = ref object of RenderProxyBox
Clips the child's painting to this render object's rectangular bounds. Backs the ClipRect widget. 
RenderClipRRect = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  radius*: float32
Clips the child's painting to a rounded rectangle of the given radius. Backs the ClipRRect widget. Backend support for rounded clipping is partial; falls back to rectangular clip on backends without it. 
RenderColoredBox = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  fill*: Color
Paints a solid fill color underneath the child. Backs the ColoredBox widget. 
RenderConstrainedBox = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  additionalConstraints*: Constraints
Adds extra constraints (via additionalConstraints.enforce) on top of those passed by the parent. Backs the ConstrainedBox widget. 
RenderOpacity = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  opacity*: float32
Wraps the child paint with pushOpacity / popOpacity so every primitive painted inside dims by opacity. Backs the OpacityWidget. 
RenderPadding = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  padding*: EdgeInsets
Insets the child by padding. Backs the Padding widget. 
RenderProxyBox = ref object of RenderObject
  child*: RenderObject
Base class for single-child render objects. Default layout passes constraints through to the child and adopts the child's size. Default paint just paints the child unmodified. 
RenderRepaintBoundary = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  subCanvas*: Canvas
  cachedSize*: Size
  cacheDirty*: bool

Caches the rasterized output of its subtree in a backend sub- canvas. On each paint pass: if cacheDirty is true, the sub- canvas is re-rasterized from the child; either way the sub- canvas is then composited onto the parent canvas in a single blit (GPU when the backend supports it).

Sets cacheDirty = true whenever markNeedsPaint is called on anything in the subtree (the bubble-up walk in markNeedsPaint passes through this render object, so we override that to flip the flag and stop the propagation).

Backs the RepaintBoundary widget. Backends that return nil from createSubCanvas skip caching entirely and just paint the subtree directly; correctness is preserved, the perf win is lost.

RenderSizedBox = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  requestedWidth*: float32
  requestedHeight*: float32
Forces tight constraints on whichever axis has requestedWidth > 0 or requestedHeight > 0. Backs the SizedBox widget. 
RenderTransform = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  translation*: Offset
  scale*: float32
  rotation*: float32
Applies a 2D translate + rotate + scale to the child's paint. Backs the Transform widget. scale = 0 or 1 is treated as identity for that axis; rotation is in radians.

Methods

method absorbPaintMark(r: RenderRepaintBoundary) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method hitTest(r: RenderProxyBox; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Default proxy hit test: forwards to the child first (if any), then unconditionally adds itself to the path so a wrapping GestureDetector is reachable. Always returns true. 
method paint(r: RenderAlign; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Paints the child at the offset set during layout. 
method paint(r: RenderClipRect; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Wraps the child's painting in save() + rectangular clipRect() + restore(). Any drawing that would extend outside this box's bounds is cut off. 
method paint(r: RenderClipRRect; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Like RenderClipRect.paint but conceptually a rounded clip. Backends that don't support rounded clipping fall back to the bounding rectangle, so children with their own rounded decoration look correct but those without may show square corners at the edges. 
method paint(r: RenderColoredBox; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Draws a solid fill rectangle covering this box's bounds, then paints the child on top unchanged. 
method paint(r: RenderOpacity; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Pushes r.opacity onto the canvas opacity stack, paints the child, then pops. Every primitive painted inside is alpha-scaled by currentOpacity * r.opacity. Nested RenderOpacity widgets therefore multiply. 
method paint(r: RenderPadding; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Paints the child at the inset offset stored in child.offset (set during performLayout). 
method paint(r: RenderProxyBox; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Default proxy paint: paints the child at this widget's origin, adding no decoration of its own. Subclasses override to draw extra layers (decoration, opacity, clipping, ...). 
method paint(r: RenderRepaintBoundary; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
method paint(r: RenderTransform; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Applies a full Translate-Rotate-Scale on the canvas around this box's top-left, then paints the child in the transformed coordinate space. Each TRS component is skipped if it's the identity (rotation == 0, scale == 0 or 1). 
method performLayout(r: RenderAlign) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                       forbids: [].}
Lays the child out with loose constraints so it picks its own size, then sizes this box to either child.size * widthFactor (when widthFactor > 0) or the parent's max width. Same for height. Finally positions the child via alignment.resolveOffset. 
method performLayout(r: RenderAspectRatio) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Picks the largest box that fits the parent constraints and has the requested width/height ratio (mirrors Flutter's RenderAspectRatio algorithm). Falls back to width-driven sizing via maxHeight * ratio when both axes are unbounded so the result is still finite. Child receives tight constraints equal to the chosen size. 
method performLayout(r: RenderConstrainedBox) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Combines additionalConstraints with the parent's via enforce (parent's tight constraints always win), then lays out the child under the merged result. With no child, sizes to the merged constraints' minima. 
method performLayout(r: RenderPadding) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Deflates the parent constraints by the inset totals, lays out the child under those reduced constraints, then sets this render object's size to child.size + insets. With no child, sizes to just the inset totals (acts as a spacer). 
method performLayout(r: RenderProxyBox) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Default proxy layout. With a child: passes the parent's constraints through unchanged, adopts the child's size. With no child: expands to fill bounded constraints (so decorative-only ColoredBox/DecoratedBox used as backgrounds, dividers and spacers don't shrink to zero). 
method performLayout(r: RenderSizedBox) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Tightens an axis when its requested dim is positive. For an axis with no requested dim and no child, the size collapses to 0 (so SizedBox(width = 12) is a 12x0 spacer, not a column-filling bar). With a child, an unspecified axis passes through the parent's range so the child can pick its own size.

Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-09 14:45:25 UTC
